advantages.notes: Jessica discovered the first-ever Legacy Advantage on Day 1 during the marooning, finding it nestled beneath the supplies scattered on the beach. The envelope revealed that if she made it to Day 36, she would receive immunity at that tribal council. If voted out before Day 36, she had to will it to another player. Jessica was eliminated on Day 30 via the season's dramatic rock draw tiebreaker in Episode 10 ('Million Dollar Gamble'). She willed the Legacy Advantage to her closest ally, Ken McNickle.

David's early idol search raised suspicion among his Gen X tribemates, but he successfully found the Takali idol on Day 5. He showed it to Ken McNickle to cement their alliance. David played it for Jessica Lewis at the Episode 4 tribal council ('Who's the Sucker at the Table?'), negating 5 votes against her and blindsiding Lucy Huang in a dramatic power shift. This bold move established David as a strategic force in the game.

Jay found the Hidden Immunity Idol at the new Ikabula tribe camp on Day 13 during the tribe expansion to three tribes. He and Will Wahl discovered it together, but they were caught by Michaela Bradshaw, who promised not to reveal their secret. Jay saved the idol until post-merge and played it at the Episode 10 tribal council on Day 30, negating 2 votes against him. While he only negated a small number of votes, the play was successful in keeping him safe that round.

After David's first idol was played for Jessica in Episode 4, the idol was rehidden. David found the rehidden idol on Day 14, demonstrating his exceptional idol-finding ability. He carried this idol through the tribe swap and into the merge but ultimately did not play it. David also used his crafting skills to create a convincing fake idol that would later fool Jay Starrett.

advantages.tribe: Vinaka (merged tribe, found at merge feast)
advantages.notes: Adam found the Reward Steal advantage hidden in the roof of Tree Mail at the merge feast on Day 21 (Episode 7, 'I'm the Kingpin'). A clue had been placed in the merge feast basket leading him to it. Adam later gave the advantage to Jay Starrett as a gesture of gratitude after Jay selected Adam to share the Family Visit reward. Jay used the Reward Steal in Episode 13 ('I'm Going for a Million Bucks') at the Final 6, stealing a steak dinner reward from David Wright and selecting David and Adam to join him.

Adam found the Vanua (Millennials) tribe's Hidden Immunity Idol early in the game. He played it on Hannah Shapiro at the Episode 11 tribal council ('About to Have a Rumble') on Day 33 at the Final 8. However, Hannah did not receive enough votes to be in danger - the target was Zeke Smith, who was eliminated. The idol play was unnecessary, making it unsuccessful.

Adam found his second Hidden Immunity Idol on Day 37 near the end of the game. He played it on himself at the Final 4 tribal council (Episode 14) on Day 38, but he received no votes - the target was David Wright, who was unanimously voted out 3-1. While the idol play was unnecessary, Adam went on to win the season unanimously 10-0-0 at Final Tribal Council.

advantages.tribe: Vinaka (merged tribe, inherited from Jessica Lewis)
advantages.notes: Ken inherited the Legacy Advantage from Jessica Lewis after her elimination via rock draw on Day 30. On Day 36 (Episode 13, 'I'm Going for a Million Bucks'), Ken opened the advantage and learned it granted him immunity at that tribal council. He played it like a Hidden Immunity Idol, and it successfully negated Jay Starrett's vote against him. Ken became the first player in Survivor history to use the Legacy Advantage. He survived and made it to the Final Tribal Council, where he received 0 jury votes as Adam won unanimously.

David Wright crafted a convincing fake Hidden Immunity Idol and planted it for Jay to find. Jay discovered the fake idol and believed it was real, giving him false confidence heading into tribal council. At the Episode 13 tribal council on Day 36, Jay played the fake idol on himself. Jeff Probst declared it was not a valid Hidden Immunity Idol, and Jay was blindsided, voted out 5-0. This was a masterful strategic play by David that eliminated one of his biggest threats.

David Wright.notes: David found two idols and successfully played one for Jessica Lewis, negating 5 votes in one of the season's best moves. He also crafted a fake idol that fooled Jay. David was voted out at Final 4 (Day 38) and finished 4th.

Jay Starrett.notes: Jay found one real idol at Ikabula and played it successfully at Final 10. He was later tricked by David's fake idol, which he played at Final 6 before being blindsided. Finished 6th.

Adam Klein.notes: Adam found two idols and played both unsuccessfully - one on Hannah (not targeted) and one on himself (not targeted). Despite misfiring on idol plays, he won the season unanimously 10-0-0 through strong strategic and social gameplay.

special_notes: Season 33 introduced the Legacy Advantage, which would become a recurring twist in future seasons
The Legacy Advantage was found on Day 1 and not activated until Day 36 - the longest any advantage was held before use
Jessica Lewis's elimination by rock draw on Day 30 (while holding the Legacy Advantage) was one of only three rock draws in Survivor history at the time
David Wright's fake idol that fooled Jay Starrett was one of the most effective fake idol plays in Survivor history
Adam Klein misplayed both his idols but still won unanimously, proving idol strategy isn't everything
The Reward Steal advantage was a new twist that would not recur in future seasons
This season featured 5 real Hidden Immunity Idols, 1 fake idol, 1 Legacy Advantage, and 1 Reward Steal - a sign of the increasing advantage complexity in the 'advantage era'
Ken McNickle became the first player in Survivor history to use the Legacy Advantage
